fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 61 uses strict inequalities (`b <= s` and `a >= e`), which allows adjacent bookings (where b==s or a==e) to pass through as non-overlapping. The tests expect adjacent bookings to be rejected.

spec.expr: all((b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) == (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) for s, e in timeline) or any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: Detect that the condition incorrectly treats adjacent windows (b==s or a==e) as non-overlapping when the tests expect them to be rejected.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s for s, e in timeline) or any(not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: When the new booking end equals an existing booking start, the overlap check should flag it as conflicting.

spec.expr: all(b__1 < s or a__1 > e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After the overlap branch is skipped (booking accepted path), verify no adjacent or overlapping slots exist using strict inequalities.

spec.expr: all(result__1[i][1] < result__1[i+1][0] for i in range(len(result__1)-1))

constraints.intent: After inserting the new window, all consecutive bookings in the result must be strictly non-adjacent (no touching endpoints).
